---
output: html_document
editor_options: 
  chunk_output_type: console
---


```{r}
# ==============================================================================
# Step 0: Environment Setup and Global Configuration
# ==============================================================================
# This block handles all dependencies and sets the master parameters for the 
# entire multi-omics pipeline. 
# ==============================================================================

# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# A. Package Installation and Loading
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
cat("Checking and installing necessary packages...\n")

# CRAN Packages
cran_deps <- c("tidyverse", "ggplot2", "reshape2", "stringr", "dplyr", "randomForest", "remotes")
for (pkg in cran_deps) {
  if (!requireNamespace(pkg, quietly = TRUE)) {
    install.packages(pkg, repos = "[https://cloud.r-project.org](https://cloud.r-project.org)")
  }
}

# Bioconductor Packages
if (!requireNamespace("BiocManager", quietly = TRUE)) {
  install.packages("BiocManager", repos = "[https://cloud.r-project.org](https://cloud.r-project.org)")
}
bioc_deps <- c("DESeq2", "WGCNA", "clusterProfiler", "org.Hs.eg.db", "enrichplot", "mixOmics", "impute", "preprocessCore", "GO.db", "AnnotationDbi")
for (pkg in bioc_deps) {
  if (!requireNamespace(pkg, quietly = TRUE)) {
    BiocManager::install(pkg, update = FALSE, ask = FALSE)
  }
}

# GitHub Packages (iRF, fastshap, shapviz)
if (!requireNamespace("iRF", quietly = TRUE)) remotes::install_github("karlkumbier/iRF2.0")
if (!requireNamespace("fastshap", quietly = TRUE)) remotes::install_github("bgreenwell/fastshap")
if (!requireNamespace("shapviz", quietly = TRUE)) remotes::install_github("ModelOriented/shapviz")

# Load Libraries
library(tidyverse)
library(DESeq2)
library(RGCCA)
library(iRF)
library(randomForest)
library(reshape2)
library(stringr)
library(WGCNA)
library(clusterProfiler)
library(org.Hs.eg.db)
library(enrichplot)
library(fastshap)
library(shapviz)
library(mixOmics)
library(BiocParallel)

# Fix for remote cluster/BearPortal plotting issues (X11 display error)
options(bitmapType='cairo')
options(stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
allowWGCNAThreads() # Enable multi-threading for WGCNA

# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# B. Global Pipeline Configuration ("Magic Numbers")
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
GLOBAL_SEED <- 42               # Master seed for all stochastic processes
DESEQ_P_CUTOFF <- 0.05           # Step 1: DESeq2 padj cutoff to target ~2000 RNA features
IRF_ITER <- 5                   # Step 3: iRF iterations for convergence
IRF_BOOTSTRAP <- 100             # Step 3: iRF bootstrap replicates for interaction stability
DIABLO_FEAT_LIMIT <- c(30, 30)  # Step 8: Number of features to keep per omics block in DIABLO

cat("Environment setup complete. Libraries loaded and parameters configured.\n")
```

```{r}
# ==============================================================================
# Step 1: Data Integration & Unbiased Alignment (Variance-Based Filtering)
# ==============================================================================
cat("Loading datasets and metadata...\n")

# 1. Load Metadata and Define Factors
meta <- read.csv("sample_sheet.csv", stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
rownames(meta) <- meta$SampleID

# 2. Load Transcriptome (VST counts)
rna_vst <- read.csv("rna_vst_counts.csv", row.names = 1, check.names = FALSE)

# 3. Load Metabolomes
pos <- read.csv("polar_pos_pqn_imputed_glog.csv", check.names = FALSE)
rownames(pos) <- make.names(pos[["mz"]], unique = TRUE)
pos <- pos[, -grep("mz", colnames(pos))]

neg <- read.csv("polar_neg_pqn_imputed_glog.csv", check.names = FALSE)
rownames(neg) <- make.names(neg[["mz"]], unique = TRUE)
neg <- neg[, -grep("mz", colnames(neg))]

# 4. Load Chemical Data (n=12 sites)
chem_raw <- read.delim("water_chemicals.tsv", sep="\t", header=TRUE, check.names = FALSE)
# Reshape so sites are rows, chemicals are columns
chem_mat <- as.data.frame(t(chem_raw[, grep("^D[0-9]{2}", colnames(chem_raw))]))
colnames(chem_mat) <- make.names(chem_raw$ChemName, unique = TRUE)
chem_mat$Site <- rownames(chem_mat)

# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# THE FIX 1: Unsupervised Variance-Based Filtering (No Double-Dipping)
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
cat("\nPerforming Unbiased Variance Filtering...\n")

# Function to filter top features by variance (without looking at labels)
filter_by_var <- function(df, top_n) {
  vars <- apply(df, 1, var, na.rm = TRUE)
  top_features <- names(sort(vars, decreasing = TRUE))[1:min(top_n, length(vars))]
  return(df[top_features, ])
}

# Select top features based on information content, not significance
rna_vst_filtered <- filter_by_var(rna_vst, 5000) # Keep top 5000 genes
pos_filtered <- filter_by_var(pos, 1200)         # Keep top 1000 pos-peaks
neg_filtered <- filter_by_var(neg, 2000)         # Keep top 1000 neg-peaks

cat(sprintf("Retained %d genes and %d metabolites for integration.\n", 
            nrow(rna_vst_filtered), nrow(pos_filtered) + nrow(neg_filtered)))

# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# THE FIX 2: Sample Alignment & Chemical Broadcasting (Solving n=12)
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Identify common samples across all biological layers
get_sample_cols <- function(col_names) grep("^(CK|D[0-9]{2})", col_names, value = TRUE)
common_samples <- intersect(intersect(get_sample_cols(colnames(rna_vst_filtered)), 
                                      get_sample_cols(colnames(pos_filtered))), 
                            get_sample_cols(colnames(neg_filtered)))
common_samples <- intersect(common_samples, meta$SampleID)

cat(sprintf("Aligning %d samples across all blocks...\n", length(common_samples)))

# Transpose biological blocks
X_rna <- as.data.frame(t(rna_vst_filtered[, common_samples]))
X_pos <- as.data.frame(t(pos_filtered[, common_samples]))
X_neg <- as.data.frame(t(neg_filtered[, common_samples]))
Y_meta <- meta[common_samples, ]

# THE BROADCAST: Link Site-level Chemical Exposure to every biological replicate
# This gives you n=150 statistical power instead of n=12
X_chem <- Y_meta %>%
  dplyr::select(SampleID, Site) %>%
  dplyr::left_join(chem_mat, by = "Site") %>%
  dplyr::select(-SampleID, -Site) # Keep only numeric chemical values
rownames(X_chem) <- Y_meta$SampleID

# Final Alignment Check
stopifnot(all(rownames(X_rna) == rownames(Y_meta)))
stopifnot(all(rownames(X_rna) == rownames(X_chem)))

# Save final outputs for next steps
save(X_rna, X_pos, X_neg, X_chem, Y_meta, common_samples, 
     file = "aligned_multiomics_data.RData")

cat("Step 1 Complete: Data is unbiased, aligned, and chemical exposures are broadcasted.\n")
```


```{r}
# ==============================================================================
# Step 1.5: Pre-Integration PCA Quality Control
# ==============================================================================
# --- Pre-Integration PCA ---

cat("Preparing combined data and running preliminary PCA...\n")

# 1. Combine the blocks FIRST
X_combined <- cbind(X_rna, X_pos, X_neg)

# 2. Run PCA (scale. = TRUE is vital here due to different omics units)
pca_res <- prcomp(X_combined, scale. = TRUE)

# 3. Create a dataframe for plotting
pca_df <- as.data.frame(pca_res$x)
pca_df$Site <- Y_meta$Site       # Correcting from REF to Site for geographic mapping
pca_df$Treatment <- Y_meta$REF   # Control, 1x, or 10x

# 4. Calculate variance explained
var_explained <- round(100 * pca_res$sdev^2 / sum(pca_res$sdev^2), 1)

# 5. Plotting
pdf("Pre_Integration_PCA.pdf", width = 8, height = 6)
p_pca <- ggplot(pca_df, aes(x = PC1, y = PC2, color = Treatment, shape = Site)) +
  geom_point(size = 3, alpha = 0.8) +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(title = "PCA of Combined Multi-Omics Features",
       subtitle = "Checking for natural biological variance before machine learning",
       x = paste0("PC1 (", var_explained[1], "%)"),
       y = paste0("PC2 (", var_explained[2], "%)"))
print(p_pca)
dev.off()

cat("PCA plot saved to 'Pre_Integration_PCA.pdf'\n")
```


```{r}
# ==============================================================================
# Step 2: Multi-Omics Integration using Supervised SGCCA (Annotated & Tuned)
# ==============================================================================
cat("Loading aligned data and annotations...\n")
load("aligned_multiomics_data.RData")

X_rna <- as.matrix(X_rna)
X_pos <- as.matrix(X_pos)
X_neg <- as.matrix(X_neg)

# --- Load Annotations Safely ---
rna_map <- tryCatch(read.delim("rna_dma_to_hsa_gn_mappings.tsv", sep="\t", header=FALSE, stringsAsFactors=FALSE), error=function(e) NULL)
pos_kegg <- tryCatch(read.delim("polar_pos_pkl_to_kegg_annotations.tsv", sep="\t", header=FALSE, stringsAsFactors=FALSE), error=function(e) NULL)
pos_hmdb <- tryCatch(read.delim("polar_pos_pkl_to_hmdb_annotations.tsv", sep="\t", header=FALSE, stringsAsFactors=FALSE), error=function(e) NULL)
neg_kegg <- tryCatch(read.delim("polar_neg_pkl_to_kegg_annotations.tsv", sep="\t", header=FALSE, stringsAsFactors=FALSE), error=function(e) NULL)
neg_hmdb <- tryCatch(read.delim("polar_neg_pkl_to_hmdb_annotations.tsv", sep="\t", header=FALSE, stringsAsFactors=FALSE), error=function(e) NULL)

combine_metab_maps <- function(kegg, hmdb) {
  if (is.null(kegg) && is.null(hmdb)) return(NULL)
  if (!is.null(kegg)) kegg <- kegg[, c(1, 3)]
  if (!is.null(hmdb)) hmdb <- hmdb[, c(1, 3)]
  if (is.null(kegg)) { names(hmdb) <- c("ID", "Annotation"); return(hmdb) }
  if (is.null(hmdb)) { names(kegg) <- c("ID", "Annotation"); return(kegg) }
  
  merged <- merge(kegg, hmdb, by = "V1", all = TRUE)
  merged$Annotation <- paste(ifelse(is.na(merged$V3.x) | merged$V3.x == "", "", merged$V3.x),
                             ifelse(is.na(merged$V3.y) | merged$V3.y == "", "", merged$V3.y), sep = " | ")
  merged$Annotation <- gsub("^ \\| | \\| $", "", merged$Annotation)
  merged$Annotation <- ifelse(merged$Annotation == "", NA, merged$Annotation)
  
  df_out <- merged[, c("V1", "Annotation")]
  names(df_out) <- c("ID", "Annotation")
  return(df_out)
}

pos_map <- combine_metab_maps(pos_kegg, pos_hmdb)
neg_map <- combine_metab_maps(neg_kegg, neg_hmdb)

# --- SUPERVISED FIX: Create Response Block & Design Matrix ---
# Convert Control, 1x, 10x into a numeric dose-response vector
treatment_response <- as.matrix(as.numeric(factor(Y_meta$REF, levels = c("Control", "1x", "10x"))))
rownames(treatment_response) <- rownames(Y_meta)

blocks <- list(
  Transcriptome = X_rna, 
  Metabolome_Pos = X_pos, 
  Metabolome_Neg = X_neg,
  Treatment = treatment_response
)

# Connect omics blocks to each other (1) AND to the Treatment block (1)
design_matrix <- matrix(
  c(0, 1, 1, 1,
    1, 0, 1, 1,
    1, 1, 0, 1,
    1, 1, 1, 0), 
  ncol = 4, nrow = 4, 
  dimnames = list(names(blocks), names(blocks))
)

cat("\n--- Sparsity Tuning (rgcca_cv) for Supervised SGCCA ---\n")
optimal_sparsity <- c(0.1, 0.2, 0.2, 1) 

tryCatch({
  set.seed(GLOBAL_SEED) # Ensure CV is reproducible
  # response = 4 targets the Treatment block
  cv_out <- rgcca_cv(blocks = blocks, method = "sgcca", response = 4, 
                     par_type = "sparsity", n_run = 5, n_cores = 1, verbose = FALSE)
  
  temp_sparsity <- if ("bestpenalties" %in% names(cv_out)) cv_out$bestpenalties else cv_out$opt$penalty
  
  if (!is.null(temp_sparsity) && length(temp_sparsity) == 4 && !any(is.na(temp_sparsity))) {
    optimal_sparsity <- temp_sparsity
    optimal_sparsity[4] <- 1 # Mathematically protect the 1-dimensional response block
    cat("Optimal sparsity mathematically found via CV:", paste(optimal_sparsity, collapse = ", "), "\n")
  } else {
    stop("CV tuning incomplete.")
  }
}, error = function(e) {
  cat("Notice: rgcca_cv encountered a formatting issue. Defaulting to fallback sparsity: c(0.1, 0.2, 0.2, 1)\n")
})

cat("\nRunning final Supervised Sparse GCCA (SGCCA) model...\n")
rgcca_out <- rgcca(blocks = blocks, method = "sgcca", connection = design_matrix, 
                   sparsity = optimal_sparsity, ncomp = c(2, 2, 2, 1), 
                   response = 4, scale = TRUE, verbose = FALSE)

# --- Map Features ---
map_features <- function(original_names, mapping_df) {
  mapped_names <- original_names
  if (is.null(mapping_df) || nrow(mapping_df) == 0) return(mapped_names)
  lookup_keys_raw <- as.character(mapping_df[, 1])
  lookup_values <- as.character(mapping_df[, 2])
  lookup_keys_x <- paste0("X", gsub("_", ".", lookup_keys_raw))
  
  for (i in seq_along(original_names)) {
    idx <- which(lookup_keys_raw == original_names[i] | lookup_keys_x == original_names[i])
    if (length(idx) > 0 && !is.na(lookup_values[idx[1]]) && lookup_values[idx[1]] != "") {
      mapped_names[i] <- paste0(lookup_values[idx[1]], " (", original_names[i], ")")
    }
  }
  return(mapped_names)
}

rgcca_mapped <- rgcca_out
rownames(rgcca_mapped$a[[1]]) <- map_features(rownames(rgcca_out$a[[1]]), rna_map)
rownames(rgcca_mapped$a[[2]]) <- map_features(rownames(rgcca_out$a[[2]]), pos_map)
rownames(rgcca_mapped$a[[3]]) <- map_features(rownames(rgcca_out$a[[3]]), neg_map)

# --- Visualizations ---
cat("\nSaving plots to RGCCA_Plots.pdf...\n")
treatment_groups <- factor(Y_meta$REF, levels = c("Control", "1x", "10x"))
pdf("RGCCA_Plots.pdf", width = 8, height = 6)

# The three sample separation plots (Ensure we only plot blocks 1:3)
plot(rgcca_out, type = "samples", block = 1:2, comp = 1, resp = treatment_groups, title = "Sample Separation (RNA vs Pos Metabolites)")
plot(rgcca_out, type = "samples", block = c(1, 3), comp = 1, resp = treatment_groups, title = "Sample Separation (RNA vs Neg Metabolites)")
plot(rgcca_out, type = "samples", block = 2:3, comp = 1, resp = treatment_groups, title = "Sample Separation (Pos vs Neg Metabolites)")

# Correlation Circle (Excluding the 1D response block for valid 2D projection)
plot(rgcca_out, type = "cor_circle", block = 1:3, comp = 1:2, title = "Multi-Omics Correlation Circle")

# Annotated Feature Weights
plot(rgcca_mapped, type = "weights", block = 1, comp = 1, title = "Top Genes Driving Component 1 (Annotated)", n_mark = 20)
plot(rgcca_mapped, type = "weights", block = 2, comp = 1, title = "Top Pos-Metabolites Driving Component 1 (Annotated)", n_mark = 20)
plot(rgcca_mapped, type = "weights", block = 3, comp = 1, title = "Top Neg-Metabolites Driving Component 1 (Annotated)", n_mark = 20)
dev.off()

# --- Extract and Save Data ---
get_selected_features_mapped <- function(model, block_num, comp_num, mapping_df) {
  weights <- model$a[[block_num]][, comp_num]
  selected_sorted <- sort(abs(weights[weights != 0]), decreasing = TRUE)
  orig_names <- names(selected_sorted)
  mapped_names <- map_features(orig_names, mapping_df)
  pure_annotations <- sapply(mapped_names, function(x) if(grepl(" \\(", x)) strsplit(x, " \\(")[[1]][1] else NA)
  data.frame(Original_ID = orig_names, Annotation = pure_annotations, Plot_Label = mapped_names, Absolute_Weight = selected_sorted, stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
}

df_genes <- get_selected_features_mapped(rgcca_out, 1, 1, rna_map)
df_pos   <- get_selected_features_mapped(rgcca_out, 2, 1, pos_map)
df_neg   <- get_selected_features_mapped(rgcca_out, 3, 1, neg_map)

write.csv(df_genes, "RGCCA_Top_Genes_Annotated.csv", row.names = FALSE)
write.csv(df_pos, "RGCCA_Top_Pos_Metabs_Annotated.csv", row.names = FALSE)
write.csv(df_neg, "RGCCA_Top_Neg_Metabs_Annotated.csv", row.names = FALSE)


```



```{r}
# ==============================================================================
# Step 3: Explainable Machine Learning (iRF) to Answer Research Question 3
# Objective: Identify which chemicals drive the biological adverse outcome
# ==============================================================================
cat("Loading aligned data for Chemical iRF...\n")
load("aligned_multiomics_data.RData")

# 1. DEFINE THE BIOLOGICAL TARGET (Y)
cat("Calculating multi-omics PC1 to use as the biological response...\n")
X_combined <- cbind(X_rna, X_pos, X_neg)
pca_res <- prcomp(X_combined, center = TRUE, scale. = TRUE)
Y_bio_response <- pca_res$x[, 1] # PC1 scores for all 150 samples

# 2. DEFINE THE CHEMICAL PREDICTORS (X)
cat("Loading and formatting chemical data...\n")
chem_data <- read.delim("water_chemicals.tsv", row.names = 1, stringsAsFactors = FALSE)

# FIX: Force the data frame into a strictly numeric matrix before transposing
chem_data_matrix <- data.matrix(chem_data) 
chem_data_t <- t(chem_data_matrix)

# Create an empty matrix to hold the chemical exposure for all biological samples
X_chem <- matrix(0, nrow = nrow(Y_meta), ncol = ncol(chem_data_t))
rownames(X_chem) <- rownames(Y_meta)
colnames(X_chem) <- colnames(chem_data_t)

cat("Mapping chemical concentrations to samples...\n")
# Map the chemical concentrations to the biological samples based on Dose and Site
for(i in 1:nrow(Y_meta)) {
  site <- as.character(Y_meta$Site[i])
  dose <- as.character(Y_meta$REF[i]) # Expected: "Control", "1x", "10x"
  
  if(dose == "1x" && site %in% rownames(chem_data_t)) {
    # FIX: Wrap in as.numeric() to guarantee it's treated as math
    X_chem[i, ] <- as.numeric(chem_data_t[site, ])
  } else if(dose == "10x" && site %in% rownames(chem_data_t)) {
    # FIX: Wrap in as.numeric() before multiplying
    X_chem[i, ] <- as.numeric(chem_data_t[site, ]) * 10 
  } 
  # If "Control" or site is missing, it remains 0
}

# FIX: Clean up any NAs that might have been introduced if there were hidden text characters
X_chem[is.na(X_chem)] <- 0

cat("\nRunning Regression iRF Model (Chemicals -> Biological Response)...\n")
# Ensure the iRF package is loaded
if(!require(iRF)) { library(iRF) }
set.seed(42) # Replaced GLOBAL_SEED with a hardcoded number for safety

# 3. RUN iRF (Continuous Regression)
irf_model <- iRF(
  x = as.matrix(X_chem),
  y = Y_bio_response,
  n.iter = 5,           # Default iterations
  iter.return = 5, 
  n.core = 1, 
  n.bootstrap = 30      # Default bootstrap samples
)

# 4. EXTRACT FEATURE IMPORTANCE 
cat("Extracting chemical importance...\n")
if ("importance" %in% names(irf_model$rf.list)) {
  importance_df <- as.data.frame(irf_model$rf.list$importance)
} else {
  importance_df <- as.data.frame(irf_model$rf.list[[length(irf_model$rf.list)]]$importance)
}

importance_df$Chemical <- rownames(importance_df)
importance_col <- if("IncNodePurity" %in% colnames(importance_df)) "IncNodePurity" else colnames(importance_df)[1]
importance_df <- importance_df[order(importance_df[[importance_col]], decreasing = TRUE), ]
top_chemicals <- head(importance_df, 20)

# 5. EXTRACT CHEMICAL INTERACTIONS
interactions <- NULL
int_obj <- irf_model$interaction
if (!is.null(int_obj)) {
  if (is.data.frame(int_obj)) { interactions <- int_obj
  } else if (is.list(int_obj) && "int" %in% names(int_obj) && is.data.frame(int_obj$int)) { interactions <- int_obj$int
  } else {
    last_int <- int_obj[[length(int_obj)]]
    if (is.data.frame(last_int)) interactions <- last_int else if (is.list(last_int) && "int" %in% names(last_int)) interactions <- last_int$int
  }
}

# --- Plotting ---
cat("Generating plots...\n")
library(ggplot2)
pdf("RQ3_Chemical_Toxicity_Drivers.pdf", width = 11, height = 8)

# Plot Top Chemical Drivers
print(ggplot(top_chemicals, aes(x = reorder(Chemical, .data[[importance_col]]), y = .data[[importance_col]])) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", fill = "firebrick") + 
  coord_flip() + 
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(title = "Top Chemical Drivers of Adverse Biological Outcome", 
       subtitle = "Ranked by ability to predict multi-omics Principal Component 1",
       x = "Organic Chemical", 
       y = paste("Importance (", importance_col, ")", sep="")))

# Plot Chemical Interactions
if (!is.null(interactions) && nrow(interactions) > 0) {
  stab_col <- if ("stability" %in% colnames(interactions)) "stability" else colnames(interactions)[2]
  int_col <- if ("int" %in% colnames(interactions)) "int" else colnames(interactions)[1]
  
  interactions$Plot_Interaction <- gsub("_", " + ", interactions[[int_col]])
  
  print(ggplot(head(interactions[order(interactions[[stab_col]], decreasing = TRUE), ], 15), 
               aes(x = reorder(Plot_Interaction, .data[[stab_col]]), y = .data[[stab_col]])) +
    geom_bar(stat = "identity", fill = "darkorange") + 
    coord_flip() + 
    theme_minimal() +
    labs(title = "Top Synergistic Chemical Interactions (The Cocktail Effect)", 
         subtitle = "Pairs/groups of chemicals that jointly drive the biological response",
         x = "Interacting Chemicals", 
         y = "Stability Score"))
}
dev.off()

# Save results
write.csv(importance_df, "RQ3_Chemical_Importance_Rankings.csv", row.names = FALSE)
if(!is.null(interactions)) write.csv(interactions, "RQ3_Chemical_Interactions.csv", row.names = FALSE)

# --- Thorough Answer: Mapping Top Chemicals to Geography ---
# 1. Get the single top chemical from your importance data
top_chem_name <- top_chemicals$Chemical[12]

# 2. Extract its distribution across sites from your transposed chemical matrix
# We'll look at the 1x environmental levels
site_distribution <- chem_data_t[, top_chem_name]

# 3. Create a comparison dataframe
geo_comparison <- data.frame(
  Site = rownames(chem_data_t),
  Chemical_Level = as.numeric(site_distribution),
  Bio_Stress = sapply(rownames(chem_data_t), function(s) mean(Y_bio_response[Y_meta$Site == s & Y_meta$REF == "1x"]))
)

# 4. Plot to show the "Distribution Relationship"
ggplot(geo_comparison, aes(x = Site)) +
  geom_bar(aes(y = Chemical_Level), stat = "identity", fill = "blue", alpha = 0.5) +
  geom_line(aes(y = Bio_Stress * (max(Chemical_Level)/max(Bio_Stress)), group = 1), color = "red", size = 1) +
  scale_y_continuous(sec.axis = sec_axis(~ . * (max(geo_comparison$Bio_Stress)/max(geo_comparison$Chemical_Level)), name = "Biological Stress (PC1)")) +
  labs(title = paste("Distribution of", top_chem_name, "vs Biological Stress"),
       subtitle = "Blue bars = Chemical Concentration | Red line = Biological Response Index") +
  theme_minimal()



cat("Done! Check your working directory for RQ3_Chemical_Toxicity_Drivers.pdf\n")
```





```{r}
# ==============================================================================
# Step 3: Explainable Machine Learning with iterative Random Forest (iRF)
# ==============================================================================
cat("Loading aligned data for iRF...\n")
load("aligned_multiomics_data.RData")

# --- DEFENSIVE ALIGNMENT CHECK ---
# Ensures the samples haven't silently shuffled out of order
stopifnot(identical(rownames(X_rna), rownames(X_pos)))
stopifnot(identical(rownames(X_pos), rownames(X_neg)))

# Re-combine just in case (Ensuring column names are unique)
X_combined <- cbind(X_rna, X_pos, X_neg)
colnames(X_combined) <- make.names(colnames(X_combined), unique = TRUE)

# Keep the 3 distinct concentration levels as an ordered factor
Y_meta$Dose_Target <- factor(Y_meta$REF, levels = c("Control", "1x", "10x"))
Y <- Y_meta$Dose_Target

cat("\nRunning Class-Balanced iRF Model...\n")
set.seed(GLOBAL_SEED)

# 1. Identify the size of the minority class (Control = 6)
min_class_size <- min(table(Y))

# 2. Run iRF with stratified downsampling
# This forces every tree to train on equal amounts of Control, 1x, and 10x.
irf_model <- iRF(
  x = as.matrix(X_combined),
  y = Y,
  n.iter = IRF_ITER, 
  iter.return = IRF_ITER, 
  n.core = 1, 
  n.bootstrap = IRF_BOOTSTRAP,
  strata = Y,                                      # Group by class
  sampsize = c(min_class_size, min_class_size, min_class_size) # 3 classes = 3 sizes
)

# Extract importance from the final iteration
if ("importance" %in% names(irf_model$rf.list)) {
  importance_df <- as.data.frame(irf_model$rf.list$importance)
} else {
  importance_df <- as.data.frame(irf_model$rf.list[[length(irf_model$rf.list)]]$importance)
}

importance_df$Feature <- rownames(importance_df)
importance_df <- importance_df[order(importance_df$MeanDecreaseGini, decreasing = TRUE), ]
top_features <- head(importance_df, 30)

# --- Mapping and Annotations ---
rna_map <- tryCatch(read.delim("rna_dma_to_hsa_gn_mappings.tsv", sep="\t", header=FALSE, stringsAsFactors=FALSE), error=function(e) NULL)
pos_map <- tryCatch(read.delim("polar_pos_pkl_to_kegg_annotations.tsv", sep="\t", header=FALSE, stringsAsFactors=FALSE), error=function(e) NULL)
neg_map <- tryCatch(read.delim("polar_neg_pkl_to_kegg_annotations.tsv", sep="\t", header=FALSE, stringsAsFactors=FALSE), error=function(e) NULL)

map_irf_features <- function(feature_names, r_map, p_map, n_map) {
  mapped_names <- feature_names
  all_maps <- rbind(
    if(!is.null(r_map)) data.frame(ID=r_map[,1], Ann=r_map[,2]) else NULL,
    if(!is.null(p_map)) data.frame(ID=p_map[,1], Ann=p_map[,3]) else NULL,
    if(!is.null(n_map)) data.frame(ID=n_map[,1], Ann=n_map[,3]) else NULL
  )
  if (is.null(all_maps)) return(mapped_names)
  
  lookup_keys_raw <- as.character(all_maps$ID)
  lookup_keys_x <- paste0("X", gsub("_", ".", lookup_keys_raw))
  lookup_vals <- as.character(all_maps$Ann)
  
  for (i in seq_along(feature_names)) {
    idx <- which(lookup_keys_raw == feature_names[i] | lookup_keys_x == feature_names[i])
    if (length(idx) > 0 && !is.na(lookup_vals[idx[1]]) && lookup_vals[idx[1]] != "") {
      mapped_names[i] <- paste0(lookup_vals[idx[1]], " (", feature_names[i], ")")
    }
  }
  return(mapped_names)
}

top_features$Annotated_Name <- map_irf_features(top_features$Feature, rna_map, pos_map, neg_map)

# --- THE FIX: Smart Label Truncation for Plotting ---
# This prevents massive lists of orthologs/KEGG IDs from ruining the ggplot margins
smart_truncate <- function(names_vec, max_ann_len = 35) {
  sapply(names_vec, function(n) {
    if(is.na(n) || n == "") return("Unknown")
    
    # Check if the string has the "Annotation (ID)" format
    if(grepl(" \\(", n)) {
      parts <- strsplit(n, " \\(")[[1]]
      ann <- parts[1]
      id_part <- paste0("(", paste(parts[-1], collapse=" (")) 
      
      # If there are many semicolons, keep only the first two
      ann_split <- unlist(strsplit(ann, ";"))
      if(length(ann_split) > 2) {
        ann <- paste0(trimws(ann_split[1]), ";", trimws(ann_split[2]), ";...")
      }
      
      # Hard cutoff for extremely long remaining strings
      if(nchar(ann) > max_ann_len) {
        ann <- paste0(substr(ann, 1, max_ann_len), "...")
      }
      
      return(paste(ann, id_part))
    } else {
       # Fallback for strings without parentheses
       if(nchar(n) > (max_ann_len + 10)) return(paste0(substr(n, 1, max_ann_len + 7), "..."))
       return(n)
    }
  })
}

# Apply truncator to create a specific column just for the plots
top_features$Plot_Label <- smart_truncate(top_features$Annotated_Name)

# --- Interactions Extraction ---
interactions <- NULL
int_obj <- irf_model$interaction
if (!is.null(int_obj)) {
  if (is.data.frame(int_obj)) { interactions <- int_obj
  } else if (is.list(int_obj) && "int" %in% names(int_obj) && is.data.frame(int_obj$int)) { interactions <- int_obj$int
  } else {
    last_int <- int_obj[[length(int_obj)]]
    if (is.data.frame(last_int)) interactions <- last_int else if (is.list(last_int) && "int" %in% names(last_int)) interactions <- last_int$int
  }
}

if (!is.null(interactions) && nrow(interactions) > 0) {
  int_col <- if ("int" %in% colnames(interactions)) "int" else colnames(interactions)[1]
  
  # Format interactions using the truncator to keep them readable
  interactions$Plot_Interaction <- sapply(interactions[[int_col]], function(x) {
    raw_feats <- unlist(strsplit(as.character(x), "_"))
    mapped_feats <- map_irf_features(raw_feats, rna_map, pos_map, neg_map)
    short_feats <- smart_truncate(mapped_feats, max_ann_len = 20) # Stricter cutoff for interactions
    paste(short_feats, collapse = " <---> ")
  })
  
  # Also store full names for the CSV
  interactions$Annotated_Interaction <- sapply(interactions[[int_col]], function(x) {
    paste(map_irf_features(unlist(strsplit(as.character(x), "_")), rna_map, pos_map, neg_map), collapse = " <---> ")
  })
  
  # We don't save the Plot_Interaction column to the CSV, keeping the data clean
  write.csv(interactions[, !names(interactions) %in% c("Plot_Interaction")], "iRF_Stable_Interactions.csv", row.names = FALSE)
}

# --- Plotting ---
pdf("iRF_Plots.pdf", width = 11, height = 8)

# Notice we use Plot_Label here instead of Annotated_Name
print(ggplot(head(top_features, 20), aes(x = reorder(Plot_Label, MeanDecreaseGini), y = MeanDecreaseGini)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", fill = "steelblue") + 
  coord_flip() + 
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(axis.text.y = element_text(size = 9)) + # slightly smaller text
  labs(title = "Top 20 Most Important Biomarkers (iRF)", x = "Multi-Omics Feature", y = "Importance (Mean Decrease Gini)"))

if (!is.null(interactions) && nrow(interactions) > 0) {
  stab_col <- if ("stability" %in% colnames(interactions)) "stability" else colnames(interactions)[2]
  
  # Notice we use Plot_Interaction here
  print(ggplot(head(interactions[order(interactions[[stab_col]], decreasing = TRUE), ], 15), aes(x = reorder(Plot_Interaction, .data[[stab_col]]), y = .data[[stab_col]])) +
    geom_bar(stat = "identity", fill = "darkorange") + 
    coord_flip() + 
    theme_minimal() +
    theme(axis.text.y = element_text(size = 8)) +
    labs(title = "Top Stable Feature Interactions (iRF)", x = "Interacting Features", y = "Stability Score"))
}
dev.off()

# Save full uncut names to CSV
write.csv(top_features[, !names(top_features) %in% c("Plot_Label")], "iRF_Top_Features_Annotated.csv", row.names = FALSE)

# Free up memory at the end of the block
rm(X_combined)
gc()
```







```{r}
# ==============================================================================
# Step 4: Linking Multi-Omics Biomarkers to River Chemistry
# ==============================================================================

# Load required libraries
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)
library(reshape2)
library(tidyr)

cat("Loading multi-omics data and chemical profiles...\n")
load("aligned_multiomics_data.RData")

# Load and format chemical data
chem_raw <- read.delim("water_chemicals.tsv", sep="\t", header=TRUE, stringsAsFactors=FALSE)
chem_mat <- t(chem_raw[, grep("^D\\d{2}", colnames(chem_raw), value = TRUE)])
colnames(chem_mat) <- chem_raw$ChemName

chem_data <- as.data.frame(chem_mat)
chem_data$site <- rownames(chem_data) 

# Combine Multi-Omics
X_combined <- cbind(X_rna, X_pos, X_neg)
colnames(X_combined) <- make.names(colnames(X_combined), unique = TRUE)

# Load Top Features
irf_features <- read.csv("iRF_Top_Features_Annotated.csv", stringsAsFactors=FALSE)
target_feature <- irf_features$Feature[1] 
top_biomarker_ids <- intersect(irf_features$Feature[1:25], colnames(X_combined))

# Build dataframe and attach Metadata
X_top_df <- as.data.frame(X_combined[, top_biomarker_ids, drop=FALSE])
X_top_df$SampleID <- rownames(X_top_df)
X_top_df <- merge(X_top_df, Y_meta[, c("SampleID", "Site", "REF")], by="SampleID")

# --- ADDED FOR RQ1: Two-Way ANOVA for Site x Dose Interaction ---
cat("Calculating Site x Dose Interaction significance...\n")
# Remove Control to strictly test environmental sites at 1x vs 10x
anova_data <- X_top_df %>% filter(REF %in% c("1x", "10x"))

cat("\n--- ANOVA Interaction Results (Site * Dose) ---\n")
for(biomarker in top_biomarker_ids) {
  # Formula: Biomarker ~ Site + Dose + (Site * Dose)
  form <- as.formula(paste(biomarker, "~ Site * REF"))
  fit <- aov(form, data = anova_data)
  p_val <- summary(fit)[[1]]["Site:REF", "Pr(>F)"]
  
  significance <- ifelse(p_val < 0.05, "*SIGNIFICANT*", "Not Sig")
  cat(sprintf("Biomarker: %s | Interaction P-value: %.5f %s\n", biomarker, p_val, significance))
}
cat("--------------------------------------------------\n\n")

# Open PDF device
pdf("Chemical_Biomarker_Analysis.pdf", width = 12, height = 10)

# --- Plot 1: Geographic & Dose Distribution ---
p1 <- ggplot(X_top_df, aes(x = Site, y = .data[[target_feature]], fill = REF)) +
  geom_boxplot(alpha = 0.7, outlier.shape = NA) + 
  geom_jitter(position = position_jitterdodge(jitter.width = 0.1), alpha = 0.5) +
  scale_fill_brewer(palette = "Set1") +
  theme_minimal() + 
  labs(title = paste("Geographic & Dose Distribution of", target_feature), 
       x = "River Site", y = "Normalized Peak Intensity") + 
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))
print(p1)

# --- Isolate 10x samples to maximize the toxicological signal ---
biomarker_site_avg <- X_top_df %>% 
  filter(REF == "10x") %>%   # Only average the high-dose samples
  group_by(Site) %>% 
  summarise(across(all_of(top_biomarker_ids), ~ mean(.x, na.rm = TRUE))) %>% 
  ungroup()

# Clean up names for matching
biomarker_site_avg$site <- as.character(biomarker_site_avg$Site)
chem_data$site <- as.character(chem_data$site)

common_sites <- intersect(biomarker_site_avg$site, chem_data$site)
biomarker_site_avg <- biomarker_site_avg %>% filter(site %in% common_sites) %>% arrange(site)
chem_data_filtered <- chem_data %>% filter(site %in% common_sites) %>% arrange(site)

# Convert to matrices for correlation
mat_biomarkers <- as.matrix(biomarker_site_avg[, top_biomarker_ids, drop=FALSE])
mat_chemicals <- as.matrix(chem_data_filtered[, sapply(chem_data_filtered, is.numeric)])
mat_chemicals <- mat_chemicals[, apply(mat_chemicals, 2, sd, na.rm = TRUE) > 0, drop=FALSE]

# Calculate Spearman rank correlation
cor_matrix <- cor(mat_chemicals, mat_biomarkers, method = "spearman", use = "pairwise.complete.obs")
cor_df <- melt(cor_matrix) %>% rename(Chemical = Var1, Biomarker = Var2, Correlation = value)

if (target_feature %in% colnames(mat_biomarkers)) {
  
  # Get top correlated toxicants
  # FIX: Sort by positive correlation to find the driver chemical!
  top_toxins <- cor_df %>% 
    filter(Biomarker == target_feature) %>% 
    arrange(desc(Correlation)) %>% 
    head(10)
  
  write.csv(top_toxins, "Top_Potential_Toxicants.csv", row.names = FALSE)
  
  # --- Plot 2: Correlation Heatmap ---
  # FIX: Plot a proper matrix of the Top 10 chemicals vs Top 4 Biomarkers instead of a single giant tile
  heatmap_df <- cor_df %>% 
    filter(Chemical %in% top_toxins$Chemical & Biomarker %in% top_biomarker_ids)
  
  # Set factor levels so the highest correlated chemical is at the top of the Y-axis
  heatmap_df$Chemical <- factor(heatmap_df$Chemical, levels = rev(top_toxins$Chemical))
  
  p2 <- ggplot(heatmap_df, aes(x = Biomarker, y = Chemical, fill = Correlation)) + 
    geom_tile(color = "white", linewidth = 0.5) + 
    scale_fill_gradient2(low = "#2166ac", mid = "#f7f7f7", high = "#b2182b", midpoint = 0, limits = c(-1, 1)) + 
    theme_minimal() + 
    labs(title = "Biomarker-Toxicant Correlation Profile",
         subtitle = paste("Top 10 positive correlates for", target_feature)) +
    theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
          panel.grid = element_blank()) # Removes grid behind the tiles for a cleaner look
  print(p2)

  # --- Plot 3: Geographic Trend Alignment ---
  top_chem_name <- as.character(top_toxins$Chemical[1])
  
  # Forced dplyr::select to prevent AnnotationDbi or MASS namespace clashes
  biomarker_long <- biomarker_site_avg %>%
    dplyr::select(site, all_of(target_feature)) %>%
    rename(Biomarker_Value = !!sym(target_feature))
  
  chem_long <- chem_data_filtered %>%
    dplyr::select(site, all_of(top_chem_name)) %>%
    rename(Chemical_Value = !!sym(top_chem_name))
  
  # FIX: explicitly assign site_levels so mutate() factor conversion works
  site_levels <- common_sites
  
  # 1. CREATE trend_data FIRST
  trend_data <- inner_join(biomarker_long, chem_long, by = "site") %>%
    mutate(
      Site = factor(site, levels = site_levels),
      Biomarker_Z = as.numeric(scale(Biomarker_Value)),
      Chemical_Z = as.numeric(scale(Chemical_Value))
    ) %>%
    arrange(Site)
  
  # 2. ADDED FOR RQ3: Add Overall Biological Stress (PC1) to the trend
  pca_res <- prcomp(X_combined, center = TRUE, scale. = TRUE)
  trend_data$Overall_Bio_Stress <- sapply(trend_data$site, function(s) {
    mean(pca_res$x[Y_meta$Site == s & Y_meta$REF == "10x", 1], na.rm = TRUE)
  })
  trend_data$Stress_Z <- as.numeric(scale(trend_data$Overall_Bio_Stress))
  
  # 3. Plot 3 including the new Stress_Z line
  p3 <- ggplot(trend_data, aes(x = Site, group = 1)) + 
    geom_line(aes(y = Biomarker_Z, color = "Metabolite (Biomarker)"), linewidth = 1.2) + 
    geom_point(aes(y = Biomarker_Z, color = "Metabolite (Biomarker)"), size = 3) +
    geom_line(aes(y = Chemical_Z, color = "Toxicant (Chemical)"), linetype = "dashed", linewidth = 1.2) + 
    geom_point(aes(y = Chemical_Z, color = "Toxicant (Chemical)"), size = 3) +
    geom_line(aes(y = Stress_Z, color = "Overall Stress (PC1)"), linetype = "dotted", linewidth = 1.2) + 
    geom_point(aes(y = Stress_Z, color = "Overall Stress (PC1)"), size = 3, shape = 17) +
    theme_minimal() + 
    scale_color_manual(values = c("Metabolite (Biomarker)" = "#E41A1C", 
                                  "Toxicant (Chemical)" = "#377EB8", 
                                  "Overall Stress (PC1)" = "#4DAF4A")) +
    labs(title = "Geographic Trend Alignment: Biomarker vs. Top Toxin",
         subtitle = paste("Biomarker:", target_feature, "| Chemical:", top_chem_name),
         y = "Z-Score (Standardized Levels)",
         color = "Legend") + 
    theme(legend.position = "bottom", axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))
  
  print(p3)
}

# --- ADDED FOR RQ2: Multi-Omics Interaction Strength Across Sites ---
cat("Calculating Multi-Omics interaction strength (RV Coefficient) per site...\n")

# Custom function to safely calculate RV Coefficient without external packages
calc_RV <- function(X, Y) {
  X <- scale(X, center = TRUE, scale = FALSE)
  Y <- scale(Y, center = TRUE, scale = FALSE)
  S11 <- X %*% t(X)
  S22 <- Y %*% t(Y)
  S12 <- X %*% t(Y)
  rv <- sum(S12^2) / sqrt(sum(S11^2) * sum(S22^2))
  return(rv)
}

site_rv_df <- data.frame(Site = character(), RV_Coefficient = numeric(), stringsAsFactors = FALSE)

# Calculate interaction strength between RNA and Pos-Metabolites for 10x samples at each site
for(s in sprintf("D%02d", 1:12)) {
  idx <- which(Y_meta$Site == s & Y_meta$REF == "10x")
  if(length(idx) > 2) { # Need at least a few samples to calculate correlation
    # Use top 500 features to save computation time
    rna_sub <- as.matrix(X_rna[idx, 1:min(500, ncol(X_rna))])
    pos_sub <- as.matrix(X_pos[idx, 1:min(500, ncol(X_pos))])
    
    rv_val <- calc_RV(rna_sub, pos_sub)
    site_rv_df <- rbind(site_rv_df, data.frame(Site = s, RV_Coefficient = rv_val))
  }
}

if(nrow(site_rv_df) > 0) {
  site_rv_df$Site <- factor(site_rv_df$Site, levels = sprintf("D%02d", 1:12))
  
  p4 <- ggplot(site_rv_df, aes(x = Site, y = RV_Coefficient, group = 1)) +
    geom_line(color = "purple", linewidth = 1.2) +
    geom_point(color = "purple", size = 4) +
    theme_minimal() +
    labs(title = "RQ2: Multi-Omics Interaction Strength Across Locations",
         subtitle = "RV Coefficient between Transcriptome and Metabolome (10x Dose)",
         x = "River Site (Upstream -> Downstream)",
         y = "RV Coefficient (0 to 1)") +
    theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))
  print(p4)
}

# Close PDF device
dev.off()
cat("Analysis complete. Plots saved to Chemical_Biomarker_Analysis.pdf\n")
```



```{r}
# ==============================================================================
# Step 5: WGCNA (Weighted Gene Co-expression Network Analysis)
# ==============================================================================
cat("Loading data for WGCNA...\n")
load("aligned_multiomics_data.RData")

# 1. Load Chemical Data
chem_raw <- read.delim("water_chemicals.tsv", sep="\t", header=TRUE, stringsAsFactors=FALSE)
chem_raw$ChemName <- trimws(chem_raw$ChemName)
chem_data <- as.data.frame(t(chem_raw[, grep("^D\\d{2}", colnames(chem_raw), value = TRUE)]))
colnames(chem_data) <- make.names(chem_raw$ChemName, unique = TRUE)
chem_data$Site <- rownames(chem_data)

# 2. Load Top Biomarker Target (from Step 3/4)
irf_features <- read.csv("iRF_Top_Features_Annotated.csv", stringsAsFactors=FALSE)
target_feature <- irf_features$Feature[1] 
X_combined <- cbind(X_rna, X_pos, X_neg)
colnames(X_combined) <- make.names(colnames(X_combined), unique = TRUE)
target_levels <- X_combined[, target_feature]

# 3. Prepare Gene Expression Data
top_genes <- head(names(sort(apply(X_rna, 2, var, na.rm = TRUE), decreasing = TRUE)), 5000)
datExpr <- X_rna[, top_genes]

# 4. Network Construction
cat("Calculating Soft Threshold...\n")
sft <- pickSoftThreshold(datExpr, powerVector = c(1:10, seq(12, 20, by=2)), verbose = 0)

softPower <- if(!is.na(sft$powerEstimate)) {
  sft$powerEstimate 
} else {
  which(sft$fitIndices[,2] > 0.8)[1] 
}
if(is.na(softPower)) softPower <- 6 # Adjusted fallback for ~150 samples

cat(sprintf("Building network with soft power: %d\n", softPower))
set.seed(GLOBAL_SEED) 
net <- blockwiseModules(datExpr, power = softPower, TOMType = "unsigned", 
                        minModuleSize = 30, reassignThreshold = 0, mergeCutHeight = 0.25, 
                        numericLabels = TRUE, pamRespectsDendro = FALSE, saveTOMs = FALSE, verbose = 0)

moduleColors <- labels2colors(net$colors)
MEs_raw <- moduleEigengenes(datExpr, moduleColors)$eigengenes

# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# THE FIX: Proper Aggregation to Avoid Pseudoreplication
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
cat("Aggregating Module Eigengenes to Site-Level (10x dose only)...\n")

# Attach Metadata to MEs and the Target Metabolite
MEs_annotated <- MEs_raw
MEs_annotated$SampleID <- rownames(datExpr)
MEs_annotated$Metabolite_Target <- as.numeric(target_levels)
MEs_annotated <- merge(MEs_annotated, Y_meta[, c("SampleID", "Site", "REF")], by="SampleID")

# Filter for 10x samples and average by Site
MEs_site_avg <- MEs_annotated %>%
  filter(REF == "10x") %>%
  group_by(Site) %>%
  summarise(across(starts_with("ME") | starts_with("Metabolite_Target"), ~ mean(.x, na.rm = TRUE))) %>%
  ungroup()

# Clean up Site names to ensure perfect matching
MEs_site_avg$Site <- as.character(MEs_site_avg$Site)
chem_data$Site <- as.character(chem_data$Site)

# Find top chemicals from Step 4
top_toxins <- read.csv("Top_Potential_Toxicants.csv", stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
top_chems <- top_toxins$Chemical[1:10] # Expanded search to top 10 as discussed!
chem_cols <- make.names(top_chems)

# Merge MEs with Chemical Data exactly at the site level
merged_traits <- merge(MEs_site_avg, chem_data[, c("Site", chem_cols)], by="Site")

# Isolate the matrices for correlation (n = 12 sites)
ME_mat <- as.matrix(merged_traits %>% dplyr::select(starts_with("ME")))
trait_mat <- as.matrix(merged_traits %>% dplyr::select(Metabolite_Target, all_of(chem_cols)))
colnames(trait_mat) <- c(target_feature, top_chems)

# Calculate correlations using Spearman and calculate p-values using the CORRECT n (nrow)
moduleTraitCor <- cor(ME_mat, trait_mat, use = "pairwise.complete.obs", method = "spearman")
moduleTraitPvalue <- corPvalueStudent(moduleTraitCor, nSamples = nrow(merged_traits))

# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Visualization and Export
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
pdf("WGCNA_Trait_Correlations.pdf", width = 8, height = 10)
par(mar = c(8, 8.5, 3, 3))
labeledHeatmap(Matrix = moduleTraitCor, xLabels = colnames(trait_mat), yLabels = colnames(ME_mat), 
               ySymbols = colnames(ME_mat), colorLabels = FALSE, colors = blueWhiteRed(50), 
               textMatrix = paste(signif(moduleTraitCor, 2), "\n(", signif(moduleTraitPvalue, 1), ")", sep = ""), 
               setStdMargins = FALSE, cex.text = 0.5, zlim = c(-1,1), 
               main = sprintf("Module-Trait Relationships\n(Spearman, n=%d Sites)", nrow(merged_traits)))
dev.off()

cat("Exporting WGCNA modules to Cytoscape format...\n")
rna_map <- tryCatch(read.delim("rna_dma_to_hsa_gn_mappings.tsv", sep="\t", header=FALSE, stringsAsFactors=FALSE), error=function(e) NULL)
annotate_genes <- function(gene_ids, mapping_df) {
  if (is.null(mapping_df)) return(gene_ids)
  lookup_raw <- as.character(mapping_df[,1]); lookup_x <- paste0("X", gsub("_", ".", lookup_raw)); lookup_vals <- as.character(mapping_df[,2])
  sapply(gene_ids, function(g) { idx <- which(lookup_raw == g | lookup_x == g); if (length(idx) > 0 && !is.na(lookup_vals[idx[1]]) && lookup_vals[idx[1]] != "") paste0(lookup_vals[idx[1]], " (", g, ")") else g })
}

TOM <- TOMsimilarityFromExpr(datExpr, power = softPower)
dimnames(TOM) <- list(colnames(datExpr), colnames(datExpr))

# --- THE ROBUST LOOP ---
for (chem in top_chems) {
  # 1. Safely extract the correlation values for this chemical
  cor_values <- abs(moduleTraitCor[, chem])
  
  # 2. Check if the chemical had zero variance (causing all NAs in correlation)
  if (all(is.na(cor_values))) {
    cat(sprintf("Skipping %s: Chemical variance is zero, resulting in NA correlations.\n", chem))
    next
  }
  
  # 3. Find the best module color safely
  best_match_idx <- which.max(cor_values)
  tox_module_color <- gsub("ME", "", rownames(moduleTraitCor)[best_match_idx])
  tox_genes <- colnames(datExpr)[moduleColors == tox_module_color]
  
  # 4. Check if we actually found any genes in this module
  if (length(tox_genes) == 0) {
    cat(sprintf("Skipping %s: No genes found in the assigned module (%s).\n", chem, tox_module_color))
    next
  }
  
  # 5. Build Dataframe and Save (Now protected from the 1 vs 0 row error)
  write.csv(data.frame(Module = tox_module_color, 
                       Trait = chem, 
                       Gene_ID = tox_genes, 
                       Annotated_Name = annotate_genes(tox_genes, rna_map)), 
            paste0("WGCNA_", make.names(chem), "_Module_Genes.csv"), row.names = FALSE)
  
  # 6. Proceed with Cytoscape export
  inModule <- is.finite(match(moduleColors, tox_module_color))
  modProbes <- colnames(datExpr)[inModule]
  modTOM <- TOM[inModule, inModule]
  dimnames(modTOM) <- list(modProbes, modProbes)
  
  exportNetworkToCytoscape(modTOM,
    edgeFile = paste0("Cytoscape_Edges_", tox_module_color, "_", make.names(chem), ".txt"),
    nodeFile = paste0("Cytoscape_Nodes_", tox_module_color, "_", make.names(chem), ".txt"),
    weighted = TRUE, threshold = 0.1, nodeNames = modProbes, nodeAttr = moduleColors[inModule])
    
  cat(sprintf("Successfully exported Cytoscape files for %s (Module: %s)\n", chem, tox_module_color))
}
cat("WGCNA Pipeline and Cytoscape export complete!\n")
```



```{r}
# ==============================================================================
# Step 6: Functional Enrichment Analysis (Gene Ontology)
# ==============================================================================
# This script takes the genes belonging to the toxic-response modules 
# identified dynamically in Step 5 and runs a Gene Ontology (GO)
# enrichment analysis to statistically identify the disrupted biological pathways.
# ==============================================================================

# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 0. Install and Load Required Packages
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
cat("Checking and installing necessary packages...\n")

if (!requireNamespace("BiocManager", quietly = TRUE)) {
  install.packages("BiocManager", repos = "http://cran.us.r-project.org")
}

deps <- c("clusterProfiler", "org.Hs.eg.db", "enrichplot", "ggplot2", "stringr", "dplyr")

for (pkg in deps) {
  if (!requireNamespace(pkg, quietly = TRUE)) {
    if (pkg %in% c("clusterProfiler", "org.Hs.eg.db", "enrichplot")) {
      BiocManager::install(pkg, update = FALSE, ask = FALSE)
    } else {
      install.packages(pkg, repos = "http://cran.us.r-project.org")
    }
  }
}

cat("Loading required packages for GO Enrichment...\n")
library(clusterProfiler)
library(org.Hs.eg.db)
library(enrichplot)
library(ggplot2)
library(stringr)
library(dplyr)

options(bitmapType='cairo')

# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 1. THE FIX: Define the Background Gene Universe
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
cat("Establishing the statistical background universe...\n")
load("aligned_multiomics_data.RData")

# Get the exact 5000 genes used in WGCNA
top_genes <- head(names(sort(apply(X_rna, 2, var, na.rm = TRUE), decreasing = TRUE)), 5000)

# Load mappings
rna_map <- tryCatch(read.delim("rna_dma_to_hsa_gn_mappings.tsv", sep="\t", header=FALSE, stringsAsFactors=FALSE), error=function(e) NULL)

if(is.null(rna_map)) stop("Cannot perform enrichment: Mapping file missing.")

# Extract Human Symbols for the background universe
universe_raw <- rna_map$V2[rna_map$V1 %in% top_genes | paste0("X", gsub("_", ".", rna_map$V1)) %in% top_genes]
universe_clean <- unique(unlist(str_split(str_trim(universe_raw), ";")))
background_universe <- universe_clean[!is.na(universe_clean) & universe_clean != ""]

cat(sprintf("Defined background universe of %d mapped human genes.\n", length(background_universe)))

# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 2. Dynamically Load Top Chemicals & Run Enrichment
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
if(!file.exists("Top_Potential_Toxicants.csv")) {
  stop("Missing 'Top_Potential_Toxicants.csv'. Please run the Step 4 chemical correlation script first.")
}

top_toxins <- read.csv("Top_Potential_Toxicants.csv", stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
top_chems <- top_toxins$Chemical[1:5]

# Loop through top chemicals
for (chem in top_chems) {
  
  safe_chem_name <- make.names(chem)
  input_file <- paste0("WGCNA_", safe_chem_name, "_Module_Genes.csv")
  
  cat(paste(rep("-", 80), collapse=""), "\n")
  cat(sprintf("Analyzing Biological Pathways for: %s\n", chem))
  cat(paste(rep("-", 80), collapse=""), "\n")
  
  if(!file.exists(input_file)) {
    cat(sprintf("Skipping %s: File not found.\n", chem))
    next
  }

  mod_genes <- read.csv(input_file, stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
  module_color <- mod_genes$Module[1]
  
  # Extract human gene symbols
  raw_symbols <- mod_genes$Annotated_Name
  annotated_only <- raw_symbols[!grepl("^Dapma", raw_symbols)]
  symbols_before_paren <- str_trim(str_extract(annotated_only, "^[^(]+"))
  gene_list <- unique(unlist(str_split(symbols_before_paren, ";")))
  gene_list <- as.character(str_trim(gene_list[!is.na(gene_list) & gene_list != ""]))

  # THE FIX: Stricter threshold for module stability
  if(length(gene_list) < 10) {
    cat(sprintf("Only %d human mapped genes found. Too few for robust GO Enrichment. Skipping.\n", length(gene_list)))
    next
  }

  cat("Running GO Enrichment Analysis (Biological Process)...\n")

  # THE FIX: Perform Gene Ontology (GO) Enrichment WITH Universe
  ego <- tryCatch({
    enrichGO(gene          = gene_list,
             universe      = background_universe,  # CRITICAL FIX ADDED HERE
             OrgDb         = org.Hs.eg.db,
             keyType       = "SYMBOL",     
             ont           = "BP",         
             pAdjustMethod = "BH",         
             pvalueCutoff  = 0.1,
             qvalueCutoff  = 0.3)
  }, error = function(e) {
    cat("Error during enrichment calculation: ", e$message, "\n")
    return(NULL)
  })

  if (!is.null(ego) && nrow(as.data.frame(ego)) > 0) {
    ego <- clusterProfiler::simplify(ego, cutoff = 0.7, by = "p.adjust", select_fun = min)
    
    if (nrow(as.data.frame(ego)) == 0) {
      cat("No significant terms remained after redundancy simplification.\n")
      next
    }
    
    clean_mod_color <- str_to_title(module_color)
    out_csv <- paste0("WGCNA_", safe_chem_name, "_", clean_mod_color, "_Module_GO_Enrichment.csv")
    write.csv(as.data.frame(ego), out_csv, row.names = FALSE)
    
    # Visualizations
    out_pdf <- paste0("WGCNA_", safe_chem_name, "_Enrichment_Plots.pdf")
    pdf(out_pdf, width = 12, height = 9)
    
    p1 <- enrichplot::dotplot(ego, showCategory = 15, label_format = 60) + 
      ggtitle(paste("GO Enrichment: Biological Processes -", chem)) + theme_minimal()
    print(p1)
    
    p2 <- barplot(ego, showCategory = 15, label_format = 60) + 
      ggtitle(sprintf("Top Disrupted Pathways for %s", chem)) + theme_minimal()
    print(p2)

    ego_readable <- tryCatch({ setReadable(ego, OrgDb = org.Hs.eg.db, keyType = "SYMBOL") }, error = function(e) return(ego))
    
    p3 <- enrichplot::cnetplot(ego_readable, showCategory = 5, colorEdge = TRUE, node_label = "category") +
      ggtitle(paste("Gene-Concept Network for", chem))
    print(p3)
    
    dev.off()
    cat(sprintf(" - Results saved to: %s\n", out_csv))
    
  } else {
    cat(sprintf("No significantly enriched GO terms were found for %s (p < 0.05) against the true background.\n\n", chem))
  }
}

```




```{r}
# ==============================================================================
# Comprehensive Multi-Omics Pipeline for Daphnia magna Exposure Study
# Addresses Research Goals Q1, Q2, Q3, and Q4
# ==============================================================================

# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Setup and Dependencies
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
suppressPackageStartupMessages({
  library(vegan)
  library(mixOmics)
  library(randomForest)
  library(fastshap)
  library(shapviz)
  library(ggplot2)
  library(dplyr)
  library(tidyr)
})

set.seed(42) # Ensure reproducible machine learning results

# ==============================================================================
# Step 1: Data Loading & Precision Preprocessing
# ==============================================================================
cat("--- Step 1: Loading & Cleaning Datasets ---\n")

meta <- read.csv("sample_sheet.csv", row.names = 1) 
rna <- read.csv("rna_vst_counts.csv", row.names = 1)
pos <- read.csv("polar_pos_pqn_imputed_glog.csv", row.names = 1)
neg <- read.csv("polar_neg_pqn_imputed_glog.csv", row.names = 1)

# Transpose omics (Samples as rows, Features as columns)
X_rna <- as.data.frame(t(rna))
X_pos <- as.data.frame(t(pos))
X_neg <- as.data.frame(t(neg))

# Parse Location and Concentration accurately
if (!"Location" %in% colnames(meta)) {
  meta$Location <- sub("(D[0-9]{2}|Control).*", "\\1", rownames(meta), ignore.case = TRUE)
}
if (!"Concentration" %in% colnames(meta)) {
  meta$Concentration <- ifelse(grepl("A", rownames(meta), ignore.case = TRUE), "1x",
                        ifelse(grepl("B", rownames(meta), ignore.case = TRUE), "10x", "Control"))
}
meta$Location <- as.factor(meta$Location)
meta$Concentration <- factor(meta$Concentration, levels = c("Control", "1x", "10x"))

# Sync Samples across all datasets safely
common_samples <- Reduce(intersect, list(rownames(X_rna), rownames(X_pos), rownames(X_neg), rownames(meta)))
X_rna <- X_rna[common_samples, ]
X_pos <- X_pos[common_samples, ]
X_neg <- X_neg[common_samples, ]
meta  <- meta[common_samples, ]

cat(sprintf("Matched %d samples across RNA, POS, NEG, and Metadata.\n", length(common_samples)))

# ==============================================================================
# Step 2 (Q1): Individual Omics Variance (PCA & PERMANOVA)
# ==============================================================================
cat("\n--- Step 2 (Q1): Omics Variance & Community Shifts ---\n")

# Combine omics for holistic PCA
X_combined <- cbind(X_rna, X_pos, X_neg)
pca_res <- prcomp(X_combined, scale. = TRUE)
meta$Stress_Score <- pca_res$x[, 1] # PC1 = Primary axis of biological variation

pdf("Q1_PCA_and_Variance.pdf", width = 8, height = 6)
pca_plot_df <- data.frame(PC1 = pca_res$x[, 1], PC2 = pca_res$x[, 2], 
                          Treatment = meta$Concentration, Location = meta$Location)
p_pca <- ggplot(pca_plot_df, aes(x = PC1, y = PC2, color = Location, shape = Treatment)) +
  geom_point(size = 3, alpha = 0.8) +
  theme_minimal() +
  ggtitle("Global PCA: Shifts due to River Extracts")
print(p_pca)
dev.off()

cat("PERMANOVA Results for Transcriptome (RNA):\n")
dist_rna <- vegdist(X_rna, method = "euclidean")
perm_rna <- adonis2(dist_rna ~ Location * Concentration, data = meta, permutations = 999)
print(perm_rna)

# ==============================================================================
# Step 3 (Q2): Multi-omics Interactions (DIABLO N-Integration)
# ==============================================================================
cat("\n--- Step 3 (Q2): Multi-omics Network Discovery (DIABLO) ---\n")

# Filter zero-variance features to prevent singular matrices
X_rna_filt <- X_rna[, apply(X_rna, 2, var) > 1e-5]
X_pos_filt <- X_pos[, apply(X_pos, 2, var) > 1e-5]
X_neg_filt <- X_neg[, apply(X_neg, 2, var) > 1e-5]

data_blocks <- list(RNA = X_rna_filt, POS = X_pos_filt, NEG = X_neg_filt)
design <- matrix(0.5, ncol = 3, nrow = 3, dimnames = list(names(data_blocks), names(data_blocks)))
diag(design) <- 0

diablo_model <- block.splsda(X = data_blocks, Y = meta$Location, ncomp = 2, design = design)

pdf("Q2_MultiOmics_Interactions.pdf", width = 10, height = 10)
plotIndiv(diablo_model, ind.names = FALSE, legend = TRUE, ellipse = FALSE,
          title = 'DIABLO: Sample Space across Omics Blocks')
tryCatch({
  # ADJUSTMENT: Lowered cutoff from 0.75 to 0.55 to reveal more biological interactions
  circosPlot(diablo_model, cutoff = 0.55, line = TRUE, 
             color.blocks= c('darkorchid', 'brown1', 'lightgreen'),
             size.labels = 1.0)
}, error = function(e) cat("Circos plot skipped (try adjusting cutoff).\n"))
dev.off()

# ==============================================================================
# Step 4 (Q3): Linking Chemicals to Adverse Outcomes (RF + SHAP)
# ==============================================================================
cat("\n--- Step 4 (Q3): Chemical Drivers of Biological Stress ---\n")

chemicals_raw <- read.table("water_chemicals.tsv", sep="\t", header=TRUE, row.names=1, check.names=FALSE)
chem_data <- as.data.frame(t(chemicals_raw[, -1]))
chem_data[] <- lapply(chem_data, function(x) as.numeric(as.character(x)))

loc_strings <- as.character(meta$Location)
chem_mapped <- chem_data[loc_strings, ] 
chem_mapped[is.na(chem_mapped)] <- 0 # Control groups have NA, set to 0
rownames(chem_mapped) <- rownames(meta)
colnames(chem_mapped) <- make.names(colnames(chem_mapped), unique = TRUE)

chem_vars <- apply(chem_mapped, 2, var)
valid_chem_cols <- !is.na(chem_vars) & (chem_vars > 1e-5)
chem_mapped <- chem_mapped[, valid_chem_cols, drop = FALSE]

if (ncol(chem_mapped) > 0) {
  rf_chem <- randomForest(x = chem_mapped, y = meta$Stress_Score, ntree = 500)
  pfun_chem <- function(object, newdata) predict(object, newdata = newdata)
  shap_chem <- fastshap::explain(rf_chem, X = chem_mapped, pred_wrapper = pfun_chem, nsim = 100, adjust = FALSE)
  sv_chem <- shapviz(shap_chem, X = chem_mapped)
  
  pdf("Q3_Chemical_SHAP_Analysis.pdf", width = 11, height = 8)
  print(sv_importance(sv_chem, kind = "beeswarm") + ggtitle("Which Chemicals Drive Biological Stress?"))
  print(sv_importance(sv_chem, kind = "bar") + ggtitle("Top Toxic/Adverse Compounds"))
  dev.off()
  cat("Chemical SHAP plots saved (Q3_Chemical_SHAP_Analysis.pdf).\n")
}

# ==============================================================================
# Step 5 (Q4 & Q5): Annotated Biomarker Discovery (SHAP)
# ==============================================================================
cat("\n--- Step 5 (Q4 & Q5): Biomarker Directionality & Visual Annotation ---\n")

# 1. Train RF to distinguish Treatment vs Control
Y_class <- factor(ifelse(meta$Concentration == "Control", "Control", "Treated"))
min_class <- min(table(Y_class))
colnames(X_combined) <- make.names(colnames(X_combined), unique = TRUE)

rf_bio <- randomForest(x = X_combined, y = Y_class, ntree = 500, 
                       strata = Y_class, sampsize = c(min_class, min_class), importance = TRUE)

imp <- importance(rf_bio)
top_features <- rownames(imp)[order(imp[, "MeanDecreaseGini"], decreasing = TRUE)[1:25]]

legacy_gene <- "Dapma7bEVm005278"
if(legacy_gene %in% colnames(X_combined) && !(legacy_gene %in% top_features)) {
  top_features[25] <- legacy_gene 
}

X_top <- X_combined[, top_features]

# 2. Retrain parsimonious model & Extract SHAP 
rf_top <- randomForest(x = X_top, y = Y_class, ntree = 500, strata = Y_class, sampsize = c(min_class, min_class))
pfun_bio <- function(object, newdata) predict(object, newdata = newdata, type = "prob")[, "Treated"]
shap_bio <- fastshap::explain(rf_top, X = X_top, pred_wrapper = pfun_bio, nsim = 100, adjust = FALSE)

# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 3. BUILD ANNOTATION DICTIONARY FOR PLOTTING
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
cat("Translating feature IDs to biological annotations for plots...\n")

safe_load_tsv <- function(file_path, col_names) {
  if (file.exists(file_path)) return(read.delim(file_path, header = FALSE, col.names = col_names, stringsAsFactors = FALSE))
  return(data.frame(matrix(ncol = length(col_names), nrow = 0, dimnames = list(NULL, col_names))))
}

rna_gn <- safe_load_tsv("rna_dma_to_hsa_gn_mappings.tsv", c("ID", "Human_Gene_Symbol"))
pos_kegg <- safe_load_tsv("polar_pos_pkl_to_kegg_annotations.tsv", c("ID", "mz", "KEGG"))
pos_hmdb <- safe_load_tsv("polar_pos_pkl_to_hmdb_annotations.tsv", c("ID", "mz", "HMDB"))
neg_kegg <- safe_load_tsv("polar_neg_pkl_to_kegg_annotations.tsv", c("ID", "mz", "KEGG"))
neg_hmdb <- safe_load_tsv("polar_neg_pkl_to_hmdb_annotations.tsv", c("ID", "mz", "HMDB"))

build_metab_df <- function(kegg_df, hmdb_df) {
  if (nrow(kegg_df) == 0 && nrow(hmdb_df) == 0) return(data.frame(Feature=character(), Biological_Annotation=character()))
  full_join(kegg_df, hmdb_df, by = c("ID", "mz")) %>%
    mutate(KEGG = ifelse(is.na(KEGG) | KEGG == "", NA, KEGG), HMDB = ifelse(is.na(HMDB) | HMDB == "", NA, HMDB),
           Ann = coalesce(HMDB, KEGG), Feature = make.names(ID, unique = FALSE)) %>%
    filter(!is.na(Ann)) %>% group_by(Feature) %>%
    summarise(Biological_Annotation = paste(unique(Ann), collapse = " | "), .groups = 'drop')
}

master_dict <- bind_rows(
  rna_gn %>% mutate(Feature = make.names(ID, unique = FALSE)) %>% filter(!is.na(Human_Gene_Symbol) & Human_Gene_Symbol != "") %>% group_by(Feature) %>% summarise(Biological_Annotation = paste(unique(Human_Gene_Symbol), collapse = " | "), .groups = 'drop'),
  build_metab_df(pos_kegg, pos_hmdb),
  build_metab_df(neg_kegg, neg_hmdb)
)

# Replace raw IDs with human-readable names
annotated_names <- sapply(colnames(X_top), function(f) {
  ann <- master_dict$Biological_Annotation[master_dict$Feature == f]
  if (length(ann) > 0 && !is.na(ann[1]) && ann[1] != "") {
    short_ann <- ifelse(nchar(ann[1]) > 30, paste0(substr(ann[1], 1, 27), "..."), ann[1])
    return(paste0(f, " [", short_ann, "]"))
  }
  return(f)
})

# Apply annotated names directly to matrices before plotting
colnames(X_top) <- annotated_names
colnames(shap_bio) <- annotated_names

# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 4. GENERATE ANNOTATED SHAP PLOTS
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
sv_bio <- shapviz(shap_bio, X = X_top)

pdf("Q5_Biomarker_SHAP_and_Interactions.pdf", width = 11, height = 8)
# The plots will now visually contain KEGG/HMDB/Human gene IDs on the Y-Axis!
print(sv_importance(sv_bio, kind = "beeswarm") + ggtitle("Top 25 Biomarkers: Directionality"))

# Specific Gene Targeting (with updated names)
actual_top_name <- names(colMeans(abs(shap_bio)))[which.max(colMeans(abs(shap_bio)))]
mapped_legacy_gene <- annotated_names[legacy_gene]

if(!is.na(mapped_legacy_gene) && actual_top_name != mapped_legacy_gene) {
  p_interact <- sv_dependence(sv_bio, v = actual_top_name, color_var = mapped_legacy_gene) + 
    ggtitle(sprintf("SHAP Interaction:\n%s\nvs\n%s", actual_top_name, mapped_legacy_gene)) + 
    theme_minimal()
  print(p_interact)
}
dev.off()

# Save Annotated CSV Reference
top_features_df <- data.frame(Feature = top_features, Importance = imp[top_features, "MeanDecreaseGini"]) %>%
  left_join(master_dict, by = "Feature") %>%
  mutate(Biological_Annotation = ifelse(is.na(Biological_Annotation) | Biological_Annotation == "", "Unannotated", Biological_Annotation))
write.csv(top_features_df, "Q5_Top25_Biomarkers_Annotated.csv", row.names = FALSE)

cat("\nPipeline Execution Complete! Check your working directory for the annotated PDFs and CSVs.\n")
```
